content.title: Comparison Test
content.content:
Suppose $0 \leq a_n \leq b_n$ for all $n$.

1. If $\sum b_n$ converges, then $\sum a_n$ converges.

2. If $\sum a_n$ diverges, then $\sum b_n$ diverges.
content.label: Theorem

content.content:
(a) The partial sums of $\sum a_n$ are increasing (since $a_n \geq 0$) and 
bounded above by $\sum b_n$. By the Monotone Convergence Theorem, $\sum a_n$ 
converges.

content.title: Limit Comparison Test
content.content:
Suppose $a_n, b_n > 0$ and $\displaystyle\lim_{n \to \infty} \frac{a_n}{b_n} = L$ 
where $0 < L < \infty$. Then $\sum a_n$ converges if and only if $\sum b_n$ 
converges.
content.label: Theorem

content.content:
Since $a_n/b_n \to L > 0$, there exists $N$ such that $\frac{L}{2} < \frac{a_n}{b_n} < 2L$ 
for $n \geq N$. Thus $\frac{L}{2}b_n < a_n < 2Lb_n$. Apply the comparison test.

content.title: Ratio Test
content.content:
Let $\sum a_n$ be a series with $a_n > 0$, and let 
$\displaystyle L = \lim_{n \to \infty} \frac{a_{n+1}}{a_n}$ (if it exists).

1. If $L < 1$, the series converges.

2. If $L > 1$, the series diverges.

3. If $L = 1$, the test is inconclusive.
content.label: Theorem

content.content:
(a) Suppose $L < 1$. Choose $r$ with $L < r < 1$. For large $n$, 
$a_{n+1}/a_n < r$, so $a_{n+1} < ra_n$. By induction, $a_{n+k} < r^k a_n$. 
Thus $\sum a_n$ converges by comparison with a geometric series.

(b) If $L > 1$, then $a_{n+1} > a_n$ eventually, so $a_n \not\to 0$, and 
the series diverges.

content.title: Root Test
content.content:
Let $\sum a_n$ be a series with $a_n \geq 0$, and let 
$\displaystyle L = \limsup_{n \to \infty} \sqrt[n]{a_n}$.

1. If $L < 1$, the series converges.

2. If $L > 1$, the series diverges.

3. If $L = 1$, the test is inconclusive.
content.label: Theorem

content.content:
(a) If $L < 1$, choose $r$ with $L < r < 1$. Then $\sqrt[n]{a_n} < r$ for 
large $n$, so $a_n < r^n$. Compare with the geometric series.

content.title: Integral Test
content.content:
Let $f$ be a positive, decreasing function on $[1, \infty)$ with 
$f(n) = a_n$. Then $\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} a_n$ converges if and only if 
$\int_1^{\infty} f(x)\,dx$ converges.
content.label: Theorem

content.content:
Since $f$ is decreasing, $f(n+1) \leq f(x) \leq f(n)$ for $x \in [n, n+1]$.
Integrating:
\[
f(n+1) \leq \int_n^{n+1} f(x)\,dx \leq f(n).
\]
Summing from $n = 1$ to $N$:
\[
\sum_{n=2}^{N+1} a_n \leq \int_1^{N+1} f(x)\,dx \leq \sum_{n=1}^{N} a_n.
\]
Thus the partial sums and partial integrals are bounded together.

content.title: $p$-Series
content.content:
The series $\displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \frac{1}{n^p}$ converges if 
$p > 1$ and diverges if $p \leq 1$.
content.label: Theorem

content.content: Apply the integral test: $\int_1^{\infty} x^{-p}\,dx$ converges iff $p > 1$.
